action.reasoning: Observation: v13 still fails (avg safe=1, max=5<<500, detector~3%, avg_dist=4.65m, 0 success); one epi reaches min_dist=0.94 but cannot sustain. Earlier v4-v5 reached ~50-100 safe_steps. Analysis: detector-conditioned 'stable follow' branch almost never triggers (detector rare), causing loss of lock on dynamic oracle target; OA sometimes interferes; gains/speed-matching insufficient for persistent 1.5m facing over hundreds of steps. Root cause: follow logic too restrictive. Literature SPF promising but prior hybrids failed due to navmesh/task config. Decision: update to v14 reactive (remove detector req for follow branch, always use compass for dist-error + strong facing k=7, base_linear=0.35 in vicinity for human speed match, refined OA only when detector<0.5, improved depth patches/valid>0, better deadzones/rotate-in-place). Rewrite complete non-truncated test_full_episode.py + predict.py with clean HeuristicPolicy v14, exact config success_angle, full per-ep diagnostics + streak, make_serializable, proper JSON contract (safe>=500 for success, all required keys, means from per_episode). Update train.py to v14. Run test_full_episode.py to validate higher safe/streak before submit_eval on predict.py (dummy ckpt).
